creator: draganj84@gmail.com
text:
Recompiled. Unfortunately it freezes again as soon as there's traffic.


On Fri, 20 May 2011 01:07:42 +0200, YongHyeon PYUN <pyunyh@gmail.com>  
wrote:

> On Thu, May 19, 2011 at 10:50:13PM +0000, Dragan Jovicic wrote:
>> The following reply was made to PR kern/157184; it has been noted by  
>> GNATS.
>>
>> From: "Dragan Jovicic" <draganj84@gmail.com>
>> To: bug-followup@freebsd.org, draganj84@gmail.com
>> Cc:
>> Subject: Re: kern/157184: [vge] Quad port Gigabit Ethernet NIC crashes  
>> kernel.
>> Date: Fri, 20 May 2011 00:19:57 +0200
>>
>>  I tested immediately on one of the servers. Preliminarily result: It  
>> works
>>  flawlessly and without any visible issues.
>>  Per your request I tunned server to boot from 12GB to 3GB; DHCP works
>>  fine, this is first time without immediate freeze. Surfing web is fine.
>>  I will try to test all 3 cards on one of machines over weekend for  
>> total
>>  of 12 ports. But so far, it seems you hit the nail.
>>
>
> Thanks for testing!
>
>>  I'm guessing the solution involves modifying [vge] driver in some way;  
>> Is
>>  there a way to fix this so that it runs on amd64 system with high  
>> amount
>>  of RAM?
>>
>
> Yes it should. I just wanted to know whether you're seeing a
> limitation of PCI based controller. Remove that tunable in
> loader.conf and try attached patch again and let me know whether it
> works or not.


--
